LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAR-02-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANTS SWINERTON BUILDERS AND FIDELITY AND DEPOSIT COMPANY OF MARYLAND'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PENDING LITIGATION. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SWINERTON BUILDERS, INC. AND FIDELITY AND DEPOSIT COMPANY OF MARYLAND, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS GRANTED. THE COURT ORDERS PLAINTIFF AND SWINERTON TO ARBITRATE ALL OF THEIR CLAIMS AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST FIDELITY TO RECOVER ON THE MECHANIC'S LIEN RELEASE BOND IS STAYED PENDING THAT ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-588347, CGC-20-588615 WITH CASE CGC-20-587352
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MICHAEL A. MAZZOCONE, Esq. of Michael A. Mazzocone, Attorney at Law (Tel: 415-399-0800) for plaintiff TRM Corporation dba Superior Tile & Stone;
Atty. DANIEL SAKAGUCHI, Esq. of Varela, Lee, Metz & Guarino, LLP (Tel: 415-623-7000) for defendant Swinerton Builders;
Attys. CLARK THIEL, Esq. and LEE BRAND, Esq. of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (Tel: 415-983-1000) for plaintiff in case no. CGC-20-588615, 875 California II, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TRM CORPORATION'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE NOS. CGC-20-588347 AND CGC-20-588615 WITH CASE NO. CGC-20-587352.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

TRM Corp.'s motion to consolidate cases CGC-20-588347, CGC-20-588615 and CGC-20-587352 is granted. The Court orders consolidation for all purposes and case CGC-20-587352 is designated the lead case and all future filings shall be made under that case number. As explained in the companion motion, the Court will compel arbitration of TRM's claim against Swinerton. The remainder of the consolidated action is stayed. The three actions involve the same projects and common issues of law and fact exist. Consolidation of the actions will further judicial economy and conserve judicial resources. The Court does not believe that a clear distinction between the pre-construction claims and construction claims can be made so consolidation is appropriate. The Court leaves arbitration joinder issues up to the parties and/or arbitrator.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Swinerton Builders And Fidelity And Deposit Company Of Maryland Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Pending Litigation
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MICHAEL A. MAZZOCONE, Esq. of Michael A. Mazzocone, Attorney at Law (Tel: 415-399-0800) for plaintiff TRM Corporation dba Superior Tile & Stone;
Atty. DANIEL SAKAGUCHI, Esq. of Varela, Lee, Metz & Guarino, LLP (Tel: 415-623-7000) for defendant Swinerton Builders;
Attys. CLARK THIEL, Esq. and LEE BRAND, Esq. of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP (Tel: 415-983-1000) for plaintiff in case no. CGC-20-588615, 875 California II, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SWINERTON BUILDERS AND FIDELITY AND DEPOSIT COMPANY OF MARYLAND's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Pending Litigation.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Swinerton Builders, Inc. and Fidelity and Deposit Company of Maryland, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The Court orders plaintiff and Swinerton to arbitrate all of their claims and the cause of action against Fidelity to recover on the mechanic's lien release bond is stayed pending that arbitration.

Based on the work order and Master Subcontract Agreement ("MSA") between Swinerton and plaintiff, the parties are obligated to arbitrate this dispute. (Swinerton's RJN, Ex. 2, sec. 15(f).) Plaintiff in fact does not contend otherwise. The Court rejects plaintiff's proposed conditions for arbitration. While the Court encourages the parties (including the owner) to globally mediate this dispute, the Court has no authority to compel mediation. (See Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, 543 [trial court lacks statutory authority to compel mediation, which is voluntary by nature].) The Court further believes that consolidation of any owner, contractor, and subcontractor arbitrations makes complete sense. Nevertheless, the Court will not order that relief/condition either. Per section 15(b) of the MSA, plaintiff agreed that: "Subcontractor further agrees to stay any action filed by the Subcontractor against Contractor until the dispute between Contractor and Owner is resolved." (Swinerton's RJN, Ex. 2, sec. 15(b).) The prime contract, which is part of the MSA per section 4 of the work order, further provides that the owner and Swinerton have "sole discretion" regarding joinder and consolidation of other claims. (Swinerton's RJN, Ex. 1 [work order], sec. 4; Mazzocone Decl., Ex. A, secs. 15.3.4.1; 15.3.4.2.) The Court will not intrude upon that discretion despite Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.3 [court "may" order consolidation]. The court further notes that Slaught v. Bencomo Roofing Co. (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 744, 751 does not compel a different result as the prime contract in that case required joinder where it is discretionary in the instant matter.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
